verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Invariants numbered with predicates; Each maps to at least one goal G1-G10; Ledger merged as normative; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gates committed red at lifeos a82c166 (5/5 failing: ledger absent), green at HEAD (5/5)
gate_result.numbering_evidence: isolation_invariant_ledger.json enumerates I01..I17 in strict sequence, each with a statement and a concrete acceptance predicate (byte-identity checks, process-tree assertions, CI-guard failures, sweep results, benchmark deltas).
gate_result.mapping_evidence: Every invariant maps to at least one goal G1-G10 and only to valid goals; the four required axes — isolation, persistence, ownership, portability — are each covered by at least one invariant.
gate_result.normative_evidence: The ledger carries normative:true, the spec's section 4 reproduces the full I01..I17 table and declares a conflicting implementation invalid, and every CT-G conformance test is defined against these invariant ids.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: tests/archbp-061-invariant-ledger.spec.ts validates numbering, predicates, goal mappings, axis coverage, and the spec merge of the published ledger.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-061 is complete: the isolation invariant ledger enumerates I01..I17, each numbered with an acceptance predicate and mapped to at least one goal G1-G10, jointly covering isolation, persistence, ownership, and portability; the ledger is merged as normative into the ratified spec (section 4) and the conformance tests are defined against its invariant ids.
